Politics.headline: Green MPs suffer drop in draft list
Politics.detail: The Greens have released the Delegate List of candidates, and some Green MPs have experienced a drop in the draft list.

Politics.headline: 'I panicked': Paul Eagle apologises as 'excessive' spending exposed
Politics.detail: The former Chatham council CEO edited quotes and contracts in a $250,000 project to upgrade his own accommodation, in what an inquiry says was "exceptionally….

Politics.headline: Luxon's targets adrift: ED times deteriorate, Jobseeker numbers climb
Politics.detail: Law and order targets are being met, but wait times in emergency departments are getting further from the mark.

Politics.headline: Republicans add to pressure on Senate majority leader after Trump voter ID bill call – US politics live
Politics.detail: Republicans join in with US president's demand for John Thune to push through Save America act.

Politics.headline: Trump administration kicks off new process to try to replace tariffs struck down by Supreme Court
Politics.detail: The Trump administration opened a new trade investigation into manufacturing in foreign countries after the Supreme Court struck down President Donald Trump's previous use of tariffs by declaring an economic emergency.

Finance & Economy.headline: S&P Global Ratings Forecasts Global Sustainable Bond Market Will Consolidate In 2026 with Issuance Levels at $800-900 billion
Finance & Economy.detail: Global sustainable bond issuance is set to level off at $800 billion-$900 billion in 2026, signaling a shift from rapid growth to market consolidation, with Europe remaining the largest market and the U.S. slowing down. Issuers are contending with rising debt maturities and shifting policy priorities.

Finance & Economy.headline: Financial Markets Committee (FMC) Statement
Finance & Economy.detail: The Financial Markets Committee noted that the ringgit ranked among the top regional performers against the USD since the last meeting in October 2025, driven by robust portfolio and foreign direct investment inflows. The FMC also acknowledged the ongoing developments in the global financial markets, particularly the recent escalation of the Middle East conflict.

Finance & Economy.headline: RWE delivers strong financial performance in 2025 and sets course for exceptional earnings growth until 2031
Finance & Economy.detail: RWE's group earnings for 2025 reached the upper end of guidance, with adjusted EBITDA of €5.1 billion and adjusted net income of €1.8 billion. The company plans net investments of €35 billion between 2026 and 2031, with the generation portfolio set to grow to 65 gigawatts.

Finance & Economy.headline: Canadian international merchandise trade, January 2026
Finance & Economy.detail: In January 2026, Canada's merchandise exports decreased 4.7%, while imports were down 1.1%, leading to a widened trade deficit with the world from $1.3 billion in December to $3.6 billion in January. Exports to the United States also decreased by 3.8%.

Finance & Economy.headline: Afreximbank unveils Season II of 'Impact Stories,' showcasing transformative projects across two continents
Finance & Economy.detail: Afreximbank launched the second season of its documentary series, Impact Stories, highlighting strategic investments and their impact on economic transformation across Africa and the Caribbean. The series showcases projects such as the expansion of the Silversands Resort in Grenada and the development of Dangote Refinery in Lagos.

Technology.headline: Iran plots 'infrastructure warfare' against US tech giants
Technology.detail: Iran has reportedly designated facilities belonging to Amazon, Google, IBM, Microsoft, Nvidia, Oracle, and Palantir as legitimate targets for retaliatory strikes, according to an Al Jazeera report citing Iran's state-affiliated Tasnim news agency, following escalating tensions.

Technology.headline: Microsoft's Copilot AI goes head-to-head with China's DeepSeek in Africa
Technology.detail: Microsoft is increasing its focus on Africa, aiming to train 3 million Africans in AI technology this year, partnering with MTN Group to offer Microsoft 365 and Copilot to its subscribers, as it competes with China's DeepSeek.

Technology.headline: Britain turns up the heat on homegrown ceramics for hypersonic missiles
Technology.detail: The UK's DSTL is investing £350K to develop domestic ceramics for hypersonic missiles.

Technology.headline: So much for power to the people – AI datacenters could jump UK grid queue
Technology.detail: Plans to prioritize AI data center connections to the UK grid are causing concerns for housing developers and billpayers.

Technology.headline: Whitehall seeks lone C++ coder to keep airport passenger model flying
Technology.detail: The UK government is offering £100K for a C++ coder to maintain software that forecasts traveler behavior at airports.

Sports.headline: Draper beats Djokovic in Indian Wells thriller!
Sports.detail: Jack Draper defeated Novak Djokovic at Indian Wells in a thrilling match.

Sports.headline: Coventry extend lead at top of Championship with convincing win against PNE!
Sports.detail: Coventry secured a convincing win against PNE, extending their lead in the Championship.

Sports.headline: India outclasses Wales, seals a World Cup berth
Sports.detail: The Indian team outclassed Wales to secure a spot in the World Cup.

Sports.headline: NBA: Kawhi Leonard's 45 powers Clippers romp over Timberwolves
Sports.detail: Kawhi Leonard scored 45 points, leading the Clippers to a dominant victory over the Timberwolves.

Sports.headline: PVL: Akari ends Creamline's win streak, claims solo fourth
Sports.detail: Akari ended Creamline's winning streak and secured solo fourth place in the PVL.

Entertainment.headline: Rihanna's Beverly Hills home hit by gunfire; suspect arrested, LAPD says
Entertainment.detail: A woman was arrested for allegedly firing multiple shots at Rihanna's Beverly Hills home on Sunday.

Entertainment.headline: Flavor Flav celebrates women athletes
Entertainment.detail: Pop culture icon Flava Flav stopped by "CBS Mornings" to discuss his plans to celebrate women Olympic and Paralympic medalists from the 2026 Winter Games.

Entertainment.headline: ‘Hannah Montana’ 20th Anniversary Special: How Miley Cyrus Will Revive Her Character in 2026
Entertainment.detail: Miley Cyrus will briefly revive her character for the upcoming Disney+ 20th anniversary special.

Entertainment.headline: Nicole Kidman and Jamie Lee Curtis adapt 'Scarpetta' book series for TV
Entertainment.detail: Nicole Kidman and Jamie Lee Curtis are working together to adapt the 'Scarpetta' book series for television.

Entertainment.headline: The Pussycat Dolls reunite as a trio for new single and world tour
Entertainment.detail: The Pussycat Dolls are reuniting as a trio for a new single and world tour.

Crime & Justice.headline: Sudan: Committee for Justice Condemns the Killing of More Than 40 Civilians in a Drone Strike on Al‑Rawaga Area in West Kordofan
Crime & Justice.detail: The Committee for Justice (CFJ) has condemned the killing of more than 40 civilians in a drone strike in Sudan, highlighting ongoing human rights concerns in the region. The CFJ is an independent association dedicated to the defense of human rights, based in Geneva, Switzerland.

Crime & Justice.headline: Failure To Examine Forensic Experts Whose Reports Are Relied Upon Vitiates Trial: Bombay High Court
Crime & Justice.detail: The Bombay High Court ruled that a trial is invalid if forensic experts, whose reports are crucial to the case, are not summoned and examined in court. The court emphasized that all steps to uncover the truth should be taken, even if the prosecution or defense fails to request it.

Crime & Justice.headline: Supreme Court Urges Union To Bring Law On Passive Euthanasia
Crime & Justice.detail: The Supreme Court of India has called for legislation to govern passive euthanasia, noting the current legislative gap requires the Court to issue guidelines. The court made these comments while allowing the withdrawal of life-sustaining treatment for a 32-year-old man in a persistent vegetative state.

Crime & Justice.headline: Man involved in Winnipeg Dollarama security guard incident facing new charges
Crime & Justice.detail: A man involved in a previous altercation with a security guard at a Winnipeg Dollarama has been arrested again in connection with a separate incident. He is accused of threatening a different security guard at the Radisson Hotel with a knife after being asked to leave.

Crime & Justice.headline: Members of the media are invited to join the Honourable Gary Anandasangaree, Minister of Public Safety, and the Honourable Sean Fraser, Minister of Justice and Attorney General of Canada, for a press conference
Crime & Justice.detail: The Honourable Gary Anandasangaree, Minister of Public Safety, and the Honourable Sean Fraser, Minister of Justice and Attorney General of Canada, will hold a press conference to discuss new tools for law enforcement and the Canada Security Intelligence Service (CSIS). These tools aim to help investigate serious threats, disrupt organized crime, and protect communities.

Education.headline: South Bay school district averts strike, reaches deal with teachers
Education.detail: A school district in South Bay, California has successfully negotiated an agreement with its teachers' union, preventing a strike.

Education.headline: Audit finds concerns with Michigan's school services program
Education.detail: An audit has revealed potential issues within Michigan's school services program, raising questions about its effectiveness and management.

Education.headline: Florida Senate votes to place new restrictions on public sector unions
Education.detail: The Florida Senate has approved legislation that would impose new limitations on public sector unions, potentially affecting teachers' collective bargaining rights.

Education.headline: Carvalho probe looms over LAUSD meeting as labor talks, charter renewal demand attention
Education.detail: Despite an FBI probe of Superintendent Carvalho, business continued at LAUSD, with a charter school denial, key labor deals and a...

Education.headline: N.B. budget: Cuts to culture funding would have 'profound impact,' advocates say
Education.detail: Advocates in New Brunswick are warning the Susan Holt government of the negative impact cutting arts and culture would have in the budget, as Nova Scotia faces anger for doing so.

Lifestyle.headline: You be the judge: should my housemate stop warming her mug and then pouring the water back into the kettle?
Lifestyle.detail: A housemate's habit of warming a mug with hot water and then pouring that water back into the kettle sparks debate.

Lifestyle.headline: Origin of deadly cancer affecting young adults revealed in alarming report
Lifestyle.detail: Colorectal cancer rates are surging among younger adults, with those 65 and under now comprising 45% of new diagnoses compared to 27% in 1995.

Lifestyle.headline: My mother's best advice: keep short accounts – in other words, forgive easily.
Lifestyle.detail: A reflection on the enduring wisdom passed down from a mother about the importance of forgiveness.

Lifestyle.headline: Dipika Kakar breaks down on Shoaib Ibrahim's vlog after cancer recurrence, says there is now a 'fear': 'What has to happen…'
Lifestyle.detail: Actress Dipika Kakar emotionally discusses her fears after Shoaib Ibrahim's cancer recurrence on their vlog.

Lifestyle.headline: Fully connected: how digital access is transforming the lives of Big Issue vendors.
Lifestyle.detail: An article discussing how providing digital access, like smartphones, is helping Big Issue vendors improve their lives.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Editorial: Transparency is a must in Columbus
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The ouster of Ohio State University's president was announced Monday morning due to using his influence over public resources to assist a private business; the board of trustees must ensure that character which puts the public before personal interests is their highest expectation.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Security Council bid nothing more than reputation-polishing
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An editorial discussing the Philippines' bid for a seat on the UN Security Council and whether it truly serves the country's interests or is merely a superficial attempt to improve its global image.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: 15th FYP: How our unique strengths serve national development
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Paul Chan, the Financial Secretary of China's Hong Kong Special Administrative Region government, discusses the 15th Five-Year Plan and its significance to China and Hong Kong, emphasizing Hong Kong's role in the dual circulation strategy and leveraging its strengths in innovation and technology.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Editorial - Epals stay out
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The government begins distributing this week the fuel subsidy to drivers and micro operators of public utility vehicles and is finalizing similar assistance for small-scale farmers and fisherfolk.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Retirement
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Pinky Concha-Colmenares reflects on her retirement from journalism after 44 years with Manila Bulletin, contemplating the loss of identity and relevance that comes with stepping away from the profession, while also recognizing it as a new beginning.

World.headline: Iran will target US bases in region unless they are closed: Khamenei
World.detail: Iran's Supreme Leader, Khamenei, stated that Iran will target US bases in the region if they are not closed, marking a major escalation in tensions.

World.headline: Trump releasing 172 million barrels of oil from reserve as prices soar amid Iran war
World.detail: President Trump ordered the release of 172 million barrels of oil from the U.S.'s Strategic Petroleum Reserve on Wednesday, after oil prices rocketed to their highest levels in years amid the U.S.'s war with Iran.

World.headline: More Than 100 School Children Were Killed in Iran. Evidence Points to a U.S. Missile Strike.
World.detail: Reports indicate that over 100 school children were killed in Iran, with evidence suggesting a U.S. missile strike was the cause.

World.headline: Israel bombards Beirut suburbs and southern Lebanon as conflict with Hezbollah escalates.
World.detail: The conflict between Israel and Hezbollah is intensifying, with Israeli forces bombarding Beirut suburbs and southern Lebanon.

World.headline: At least 17 killed after drone strikes school in Sudan.
World.detail: A drone strike on a school in Sudan has resulted in the death of at least 17 people.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zanu PF rolls out new arguments to back Mnangagwa term extension
Zimbabwe.detail: Zanu PF is advancing fresh legal arguments to justify a constitutional amendment that could allow President Emmerson Mnangagwa to remain in office beyond 2028, arguing that it doesn't require a referendum. The ruling party's push faces resistance from opposition and civil society who argue altering the presidential term without public approval undermines constitutional safeguards.

Zimbabwe.headline: Deputy Minister Kwidini assesses progress of Mpilo Hospital upgrades
Zimbabwe.detail: Deputy Minister Kwidini has assessed the progress of the Mpilo Hospital upgrades. The Mpilo Central Hospital is located in Bulawayo, Zimbabwe's second-largest city.

Zimbabwe.headline: Govt unveils knowledge economy incentives to become global outsourcing hub
Zimbabwe.detail: The government has unveiled knowledge economy incentives aimed at transforming the country into a global outsourcing hub. This initiative seeks to leverage Zimbabwe's human capital and technological infrastructure to attract international business process outsourcing (BPO) operations.

Zimbabwe.headline: Miners4ED craft mining strategy to support Vision 2030
Zimbabwe.detail: Miners4ED, an association of miners, is developing a mining strategy to contribute to the achievement of Vision 2030, a national development plan aimed at transforming Zimbabwe into an upper-middle-income economy by 2030. This strategy likely involves enhancing mining output, promoting value addition, and ensuring sustainable mining practices.

Zimbabwe.headline: Retired Generals demand referendum on controversial constitutional changes
Zimbabwe.detail: A group of retired military leaders and former liberation fighters in Zimbabwe has urged Parliament to submit the proposed Constitutional Amendment Bill No. 3 of 2026 to a national referendum. This bill is related to the extension of presidential terms.
